title: Effects of two versus three weekly sessions of low-load elastic band resistance training on functional fitness and metabolic biomarkers in older adults: a randomized controlled trial.
abstract: Resistance training is recognized as an effective way to improve muscle strength, metabolic health, and functional fitness in older adults. However, there is little agreement on the ideal number of resistance training sessions per week. It is important to clarify the comparative effects of various training frequencies, especially with accessible low-load methods like elastic bands, to create practical and effective exercise recommendations for this population.
